trans_title: Haruki Murakami, de Underground à 1Q84 - L'attentat de la secte Aum
Haruki Murakami, von Untergrundkrieg bis 1Q84 - Das Attentat, die Sekte und der Schriftsteller
introduction: In March 1995, the sarin gas attack on the Tokyo subway, carried out by members of the Aum sect, shocked Japan and the world. Dozens were killed, and thousands injured. This horrific event deeply impacted Japanese society and left a lasting mark on writer Haruki Murakami, who returned to his homeland after several years abroad, profoundly affected by the tragedy.In Underground, Murakami investigates the reasons behind individuals joining the Aum sect, gathering testimonies from victims and former members. The documentary explores taboo aspects of Japanese society and marks a turning point in Murakami’s career. It inspired his major work 1Q84, where he delves into themes of cult influence and the search for truth, blending reality with fiction. Through its carefully crafted aesthetic, the film underscores his exploration of the social psyche, a theme that resonates throughout his writing.
